questionText: My brother wants to give up sugar because it's bad for his health. What does 'give up' mean?
explanation: Le phrasal verb 'give up' signifie 'abandonner' ou 'arrêter de faire quelque chose'. Ici, il s'agit d'arrêter de consommer du sucre.

questionText: The teacher asked us to look up the new words in a dictionary. What is the meaning of 'look up'?
explanation: 'Look up' signifie 'chercher une information' (souvent dans un dictionnaire, une encyclopédie ou sur internet).

questionText: We have to turn off the lights before leaving the classroom. 'Turn off' is the opposite of:
explanation: 'Turn off' = éteindre. 'Switch on' ou 'turn on' = allumer. C'est donc l'antonyme direct.

questionText: Unfortunately, they had to put off the meeting until next week. Which phrasal verb can replace 'put off'?
explanation: 'Put off' signifie 'reporter à plus tard', c'est-à-dire 'postpone'. Ce n'est pas annuler (cancel), mais décaler dans le temps.

questionText: The phone is ringing. Can you please pick up? What does 'pick up' mean here?
explanation: Avec un téléphone, 'pick up' signifie 'décrocher' ou 'répondre'. Dans un autre contexte, cela peut signifier 'aller chercher quelqu'un'.

questionText: She ran into her former classmate at the supermarket yesterday. What is the best synonym for 'ran into'?
explanation: 'Run into' signifie 'rencontrer par hasard', 'tomber sur quelqu'un' de manière inattendue, sans planification.

questionText: The new government wants to carry out several reforms before the end of the year. What does 'carry out' mean?
explanation: 'Carry out' signifie 'mettre à exécution', 'réaliser' ou 'effectuer' une tâche, un ordre ou un projet.

questionText: I can't hear the music. Could you please turn it up? The pronoun 'it' refers to the music. 'Turn up' means:
explanation: 'Turn up' le volume signifie 'augmenter le volume' (monter le son). Le contraire est 'turn down' (baisser).

questionText: The firemen managed to put out the fire after three hours. What is the meaning of 'put out'?
explanation: 'Put out' un incendie signifie 'éteindre' (extinguish). On utilise aussi 'put out' pour une lumière ou une cigarette.

questionText: Don't throw away that bottle! We can use it up for the science experiment. 'Use up' means:
explanation: 'Use up' signifie 'utiliser entièrement', 'épuiser' une ressource (jusqu'à la dernière goutte ou le dernier morceau).

questionText: He looks down on his classmates because he gets good grades. This phrasal verb has a ______ connotation.
explanation: 'Look down on' signifie 'mépriser', 'regarder de haut'. C'est très négatif, car cela exprime un sentiment de supériorité.

questionText: I was very sick, but I got over the flu in one week. What does 'get over' mean in this context?
explanation: 'Get over' peut signifier 'se remettre de' (une maladie, une émotion). Ici, c'est la guérison physique après la grippe.

questionText: Please fill in this application form with your personal details. 'Fill in' means:
explanation: 'Fill in' ou 'fill out' signifie 'remplir' (un formulaire, un document) en écrivant les informations demandées.

questionText: The meeting broke out in chaos when the results were announced. Here, 'broke out' means:
explanation: 'Break out' pour un événement signifie 'éclater soudainement', souvent de manière violente ou agitée (guerre, feu, bagarre).

questionText: I can't make out what is written on the board. The letters are too small. 'Make out' here means:
explanation: 'Make out' peut signifier 'distinguer' (avec les yeux ou les oreilles) ou 'comprendre'. Ici, l'élève ne distingue pas les lettres.

questionText: Despite the difficulties, the team decided to go on with the project. Which option best replaces 'go on'?
explanation: 'Go on' signifie 'continuer' (à faire quelque chose). C'est l'idée de persévérer malgré les obstacles.

questionText: The children were told to look after their younger brother while the parents were away. 'Look after' means:
explanation: 'Look after' signifie 's'occuper de', 'prendre soin de' quelqu'un ou quelque chose (un enfant, un animal, une maison).

questionText: He promised to call off the strike if the government agreed to negotiate. What does 'call off' mean?
explanation: 'Call off' signifie 'annuler' un événement prévu (grève, réunion, mariage, etc.).

questionText: We set off at dawn to avoid the traffic jam. In this sentence, 'set off' means:
explanation: 'Set off' peut signifier 'partir' (entamer un voyage). On utilise aussi 'set out' dans le même sens.

questionText: The company turned down my job application. I am very disappointed. 'Turned down' means:
explanation: 'Turn down' une offre ou une demande signifie 'rejeter', 'refuser'. C'est le contraire de 'accept' (accepter).
